extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, whose condition requires hyperkalemia, bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, asthma, g6pd_deficiency, and immunosuppressed all true. rec_drug_b is set true only by r2, whose condition is hyperkalemia. r1 targets output a and r2 targets output b, so they resolve independently with no priority conflict between them. Crucially, r1's condition entails r2's condition (both require hyperkalemia), so any input that triggers rec_drug_a also triggers rec_drug_b. Setting all six of r1's conjuncts true (hyperkalemia, bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, asthma, g6pd_deficiency, immunosuppressed) fires both r1 (rec_drug_a := true) and r2 (rec_drug_b := true). The default only applies to outputs left unset, so it does not override these. Thus rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. anticoagulated is irrelevant to a and b (set false here).
